text.title: Stakeholder Register Rules and Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Rules for maintaining register accuracy, evidence-backed attitudes, PII handling, and safe agentic curation; anti-patterns that make the register misleading or a liability.

rule.statement: Refresh the register at minimum quarterly; monthly during high-volatility periods (post-merger, leadership change, regulator activity); an unrefreshed register is wrong, not just old.
rule.rationale: Stakeholder power and attitude change with organizational events; a stale register leads the PM to engage the wrong people at the wrong intensity, wasting effort and missing real risks.

rule.statement: Every attitude assertion (+/-) must reference at least one behavioral signal (attendance pattern, response time, escalation, direct quote); attitude defaults to "unknown" without evidence.
rule.rationale: Self-reported or assumed attitude is unreliable; observed behavior is more predictive. Requiring evidence also forces the PM to have real conversations rather than guessing.

rule.statement: Triangulate attitude from at least two independent signals before updating from "unknown"; a single data point is noise.
rule.rationale: Cross-cultural communication differences, tone misread in email, and politeness norms all produce false signals; corroboration reduces misclassification.

rule.statement: Treat the register as confidential: store in a private repo, run a PII scrubber pre-commit to block candid commentary and salary data, encrypt at rest if shared beyond the immediate team.
rule.rationale: Registers contain candid assessments of individuals' attitudes and influence; leaked registers damage relationships and may constitute GDPR Article 9 violations if attitude implies political opinion or health status.

antipattern.description: Including candid negative commentary about a stakeholder in a machine-readable field that is passed to third-party LLMs or shared in team channels.
antipattern.reason: Machine-readable candid data is one copy-paste or API call away from the subject; it damages trust, creates legal liability, and inhibits honest assessment in future registers.

section.title: Agentic Curation

rule.statement: The register-curator agent must validate every name against an authoritative directory before proposing an add or update; reject names absent from the source.
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate plausible names from titles; a register populated with invented people is worse than no register — it creates false confidence in coverage.

rule.statement: Cap agent edit batches at 10 patches per run; require human review on the diff before merge; never allow the agent to deprecate stakeholders autonomously.
rule.rationale: Bulk automated reclassification destroys historical signal and may remove stakeholders who are still active but temporarily quiet; human review preserves context the agent cannot infer.

section.reference: Register completeness and evidence audit script
YAML template for a single stakeholder entry

antipattern.description: Maintaining a single register with more than 50-100 entries without partitioning by category or org unit.
antipattern.reason: Registers above 50 entries become unusable for active engagement planning; the PM dilutes attention across too many relationships. Partition by category (Sponsor, Regulator, User, Vendor) and operate on slices.
